title: melaleuca calcicola
intro: Melaleuca calcicola, also known as the coastal tea tree, is a small shrub native to Western Australia. The medicinal properties of Melaleuca calcicola are attributed to its essential oils, particularly cineole and limonene, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. This plant has been traditionally used to aid in the relief of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from coughs and colds. Melaleuca calcicola is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, salves, and oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
